principles.name: NORMATIVE_AND_INFORMATIVE_GUIDANCE_MUST_BE_SEPARATED
principles.statement: Conformance requirements must be derived from normative WCAG or WAI-ARIA text; Understanding documents, APG, ACT rules and educational guidance may explain or operationalize requirements but must not be presented as additional normative obligations.
tests: Every accessibility requirement records source class
No informative recommendation is labeled mandatory unless another authority makes it so
anti_patterns: Treating APG examples as law
Treating advisory techniques as required for WCAG conformance

principles.name: AUTOMATION_CANNOT_ESTABLISH_ACCESSIBILITY_ALONE
principles.statement: Automated tools may detect selected failures and support repeatability, but accessibility and conformance require knowledgeable human evaluation and, where appropriate, user evaluation.
tests: Test plan distinguishes automated, semi-automated and manual checks
Report states uncovered criteria and human-judgment dependencies
anti_patterns: Declaring WCAG conformance from a zero-error scanner report
Treating tool coverage as criterion coverage

principles.name: NATIVE_SEMANTICS_BEFORE_ARIA
principles.statement: Use native HTML semantics and behavior when they satisfy the requirement; introduce ARIA only when necessary and implement the associated state, property and keyboard contracts completely.
tests: Role, name, state, value and keyboard behavior verified
anti_patterns: Clickable div with role button but no keyboard support
ARIA used to mask invalid interaction architecture

principles.name: KEYBOARD_ACCESS_IS_A_BEHAVIORAL_CONTRACT
principles.statement: Every interactive function must be operable through a coherent keyboard model with visible focus, predictable movement, escape and recovery behavior appropriate to the component.
tests: Composite widget keys match documented pattern
anti_patterns: Tab reaches controls but actions require drag
Focus moved without user context

principles.name: ACCESSIBLE_NAME_ROLE_STATE_AND_VALUE_MUST_MATCH_REAL_BEHAVIOR
principles.statement: The accessibility tree must expose a name, role, state and value that accurately represent the visible control and update when the control changes.
tests: Screen-reader announcement matches visible label and state
Dynamic changes update exposed state without redundant noise
anti_patterns: Visual toggle announced as generic button
Stale aria-expanded or aria-checked value

principles.name: REFLOW_ZOOM_AND_TEXT_RESIZING_ARE_LAYOUT_REQUIREMENTS
principles.statement: Content and controls must remain readable, operable and logically ordered under required zoom, reflow and text-spacing conditions without loss of information or functionality.
tests: 400 percent zoom and reflow at applicable viewport
No two-dimensional scrolling except permitted content
anti_patterns: Fixed-height cards clip enlarged text
Zoom reveals inaccessible off-canvas controls

principles.name: POINTER_INTERACTION_REQUIRES_NON_DRAG_AND_TARGET_ALTERNATIVES
principles.statement: Pointer interactions must account for limited dexterity by providing non-drag alternatives where required, adequate target sizing and cancellation or recovery from accidental activation.
anti_patterns: Sorting available only by drag
Tiny adjacent icon-only controls without spacing

principles.name: ERRORS_REQUIRE_IDENTIFICATION_GUIDANCE_AND_RECOVERY
principles.statement: Errors must be identifiable without relying only on color, associated with the affected input, explained in actionable language and recoverable without unnecessary data loss.
tests: High-impact submissions include review or reversal as applicable
anti_patterns: Red border only
Generic invalid input message
Form reset after one error

principles.name: ACCESSIBILITY_TESTING_REQUIRES_A_RECORDED_ENVIRONMENT
principles.statement: Assistive-technology results are valid only within a recorded matrix of operating system, browser, assistive technology, versions, settings, input mode, task and expected outcome.
anti_patterns: Pass based on an unnamed screen reader
Generalizing one browser and AT pairing to all users

principles.name: CONFORMANCE_AND_USABILITY_ARE_COMPLEMENTARY_NOT_EQUIVALENT
principles.statement: Standards evaluation determines whether specified requirements are met; evaluation with disabled users reveals real-world usability and unmet needs. Neither result substitutes for the other.
tests: Report separates criterion failures from usability findings
User findings do not become universal claims without scope
Conformance claim does not imply absence of barriers
anti_patterns: One successful user session proves conformance
WCAG AA claim treated as proof of excellent experience
